macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Indices Close Lower Amid Banking Sector Weakness
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 6878.88, Nasdaq dropped 0.9% to 22668.21, and DJIA declined 0.98% to 48977.92 on Friday, with banks underperforming as the KBW Nasdaq Bank Index plunged 4.85%. Concerns over credit risks were heightened by the collapse of UK financing firm Market Financial Solutions.

items.title: ASX 200 Ends February Strongly, Materials Sector Surges
items.summary: Australia's ASX 200 closed at 9198.60, up 1.29% for the week and 3.72% for February, driven by solid earnings from major miners with the Materials sector gaining 8.97% in the month.

items.title: Asian Markets Open Sharply Lower on Monday
items.summary: Shanghai Composite opened down 0.27% at 4151.80, Shenzhen down 1.16%, Hang Seng down 1.22%, ASX 200 down 0.42% at 9159.90, Nikkei down 1.49%, and Straits Times down 1.65%, reflecting weak global cues.

items.title: Indian Nifty 50 and Sensex Plunge Over 2% at Open
items.summary: Nifty 50 fell 2.06% and Sensex dropped 3.38% at market open amid U.S.-Iran war tensions, rising crude oil prices, FII selling, and weak global markets.

items.title: Key US Economic Data Ahead: Jobs Report, ISM, and Fed Beige Book
items.summary: This week features February jobs data expecting +60k gains and 4.3% unemployment, Manufacturing ISM on Monday (proj. 51.5), Services ISM Wednesday, and Fed Beige Book for labor and inflation insights.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips to $63,000 Amid Geopolitical Tensions but Shows Resilience
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to a low of $63,000 due to escalating geopolitical conflicts over the weekend, with a 2.21% decline in the past 24 hours below $67,000, while Ethereum dropped 3.92% below $2,000. Markets demonstrated strong resistance despite events in Iran, positioning crypto as a potential safe haven.

items.title: Uniswap Launches Multi-Chain Fee-Sharing Governance Vote
items.summary: Uniswap initiated a vote to redirect at least one-sixth of transaction fees from certain networks into a 'token jar' for UNI holders who burn equivalent UNI tokens. This marks a significant governance shift for the DEX protocol.

items.title: Aave Passes 'Aave Will Win' Proposal for Token-Centric Model
items.summary: The Aave governance vote for the 'Aave Will Win' Temp Check passed, aiming to allocate 100% of product revenue directly to AAVE token holders, transitioning toward a fully token-centric model.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin Outlines Ethereum's Two-Phase Scalability Roadmap
items.summary: Ethereum's roadmap includes short-term multidimensional gas to combat state bloat and long-term zkEVM integration with Data Availability Sampling for 1,000x throughput boost. This evolves Ethereum into a efficient global settlement layer.

items.title: Major Institutions Expand Crypto Services: SoFi, Morgan Stanley, Citi
items.summary: U.S. bank SoFi now supports Solana deposits; Morgan Stanley applied for a trust bank license for Bitcoin custody, trading, and staking; Citi plans institutional Bitcoin custody in 2026.

items.title: New Stablecoin Launches: MoonPay's PYUSDx and SBI's JPYSC
items.summary: MoonPay unveiled PYUSDx, a framework for apps to issue stablecoins backed by PayPal's PYUSD; SBI Holdings announced JPYSC, a yen-backed stablecoin.

items.title: Bitcoin Undervalued at 24-66% Below Historical Trends vs. Gold
items.summary: Samson Mow states Bitcoin trades 24%–66% below its trend compared to gold (now over $5,360 for PAXG) and global money supply, suggesting potential reversal as gold surges.

items.title: Broad Crypto Market Decline Led by SocialFi Sector
items.summary: The overall market fell, with SocialFi down 6.25% (TON -6.97%, CHZ -4.16%), Layer 2 -2.71% (STRK -6.06%), and others; DeFi saw Hyperliquid (HYPE) rise 3.51%.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AI Breakthroughs in Decoding Human Thoughts and Brain Activity
items.summary: Researchers are using machine learning to decode neural signals, reconstruct visual and auditory experiences from brain scans, and develop brain-computer interfaces. Companies like Neuralink are moving these technologies toward commercial deployment within the next few years.

items.title: Physical AI Emerges as Next Major Transformation Beyond Generative AI
items.summary: A new phase of AI is extending intelligence into the physical world through robots, autonomous systems, and intelligent machines. Shenzhen is positioning itself as a global capital for Physical AI with real-world deployment across manufacturing, logistics, and urban infrastructure.

items.title: Machine Learning Redefines Marketing Performance and ROI Measurement
items.summary: AI and machine learning are becoming essential for forecasting customer intent, personalizing content, and automating marketing operations. Future developments include autonomous campaign generation, predictive commerce, and real-time voice analytics for sales optimization.

items.title: Japan Accelerates AI Adoption Despite Low Workplace Implementation
items.summary: Japan's government and corporations are aggressively promoting AI deployment, with SoftBank committing $3 billion annually to OpenAI technology and the government allocating ¥340 billion in digital transformation subsidies. However, surveys reveal shallow workplace adoption and concerns about AI accuracy and reliability.

items.title: Bidgely Launches UtilityAI Pro, First Vertical AI Platform for Energy Sector
items.summary: Bidgely is showcasing UtilityAI Pro at the IDC European Utilities Xchange, positioning it as the industry's first vertical AI platform designed to transform utilities through enhanced data granularity and grid optimization.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokémon Company Announces Next-Gen Pokémon Winds and Pokémon Waves
items.summary: The Pokémon Company revealed Pokémon Winds and Pokémon Waves, the first two games of its next generation (X), set in an open-world environment. This marks a significant step into open-world Pokémon gameplay.

items.title: Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy Launches on Mobile This Month
items.summary: Square Enix announced Dissidia Duellum Final Fantasy, a 3v3 team boss battle arena game set in the Final Fantasy universe, now available for pre-registration on mobile with a launch later in March.

items.title: Rhythm Game Stage Tour Announced for PC and More Platforms
items.summary: RedOctane Games, led by rhythm game veterans, revealed Stage Tour, blending classic note-highway gameplay like Guitar Hero with modern twists, set for PC release this fall.

items.title: Over 35 New Games Launch on Xbox Next Week Including Marathon and Planet of Lana II
items.summary: Xbox sees 36 new releases next week, highlighted by Marathon, Planet of Lana II, and Legacy of Kain: Defiance Remastered, boosting the platform's early March lineup.

items.title: Five New Steam Games Spotlighted: Fear Effect 2 PC Release and More
items.summary: PC Gamer highlights recent Steam releases like Fear Effect 2: Retro Helix (PS1 classic now on PC with QoL updates), New Heights (limb-control climbing sim), and Emberbane (metroidvania).

items.title: Major March 2026 Releases Confirmed: Pokémon Pokopia, Fatal Frame II Remake, Monster Hunter Stories 3
items.summary: Upcoming games for March include Pokémon Pokopia on March 5, Fatal Frame II: Crimson Butterfly Remake on March 12, and Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection on March 13, across Switch 2 and other platforms.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Lenovo Unveils Foldable Gaming Handheld and Modular AI PC Concept at MWC 2026
items.summary: Lenovo announced a wild foldable gaming handheld, Modular AI PC concept with swappable ports and dual displays, and adaptive AI devices ahead of MWC 2026 starting March 2. The concepts aim to create flexible computing systems for various users including gamers and professionals.[1][2]

items.title: Honor Reveals Robot Phone with 200MP Gimbal Camera at MWC 2026
items.summary: Honor showcased its Robot Phone concept featuring a 200-megapixel primary camera with three-axis gimbal stabilization and modes like Super Steady Video, planned for launch later in 2026. The device was highlighted among early MWC announcements.[1]

items.title: Fujitsu Launches Uvance for Retail Cloud Solution
items.summary: Fujitsu introduced Uvance for Retail on March 2, 2026, a secure cloud platform integrating retail data and operations to boost decision-making and sustainable growth for Japanese retailers. It will be demoed at RetailTech JAPAN 2026 with causal analysis and multi-agent features.[5]

items.title: AWS UAE Data Center Fire Causes Temporary Cloud Disruption
items.summary: A fire at an AWS data center in the UAE triggered temporary cloud service disruptions, as reported in tech news on March 2, 2026. The incident highlights ongoing challenges in data center reliability.[4]
